title: Hypertext Knowledge Base Service
description: A service providing context-specific suggestions based on a pilot-specific Knowledge Base (KB). The KB is constructed from pilot-specific datasets and can evolve through expert input, allowing users to contribute to and expand the knowledge over time. Given a defined context (e.g. a selected asset, keyword or annotation), the service offers related content or concepts to support user exploration, enrichment and research. The scope and quality of the KB depends directly on the availability, accessibility and semantic richness of the incorporated datasets. Accessible via a backend REST API integrated with a knowledge graph (e.g. graph database).

access_points.notes: Backend service accessible via REST API. Integration with a knowledge graph (e.g. graph database). Access to pilot datasets or endpoints for context generation.

intended_for: Heritage professionals, researchers and curators requiring contextual discovery and exploration of related heritage information
RHDT pilot users seeking non-obvious connections between assets, annotations and knowledge entities

used_for.activity_label: operation_05 — Spatiotemporal Data Visualisation and VR/AR Experience: surfacing related heritage content and concepts to support expert users in building evidence-based conservation strategies and contextualising the Japanese Tower's materials and history within the RHDT knowledge base

has_functionality: Context-specific suggestions based on a pilot-specific KB
Expert-driven KB evolution and expansion over time
Related content or concept surfacing given a defined context (selected asset, keyword, annotation)
Support for user exploration, enrichment and research
